title: Special and additional needs education teaching professionals
description: Advise and assist students and provide educational and vocational guidance services. Teach academic, social, and life skills to preschool-aged students with learning, emotional, or physical disabilities. Includes teachers who specialize and work with students who are blind or have visual impairments; students who are deaf or have hearing impairments; and students with intellectual disabilities. Teach academic, social, and life skills to kindergarten students with learning, emotional, or physical disabilities. Includes teachers who specialize and work with students who are blind or have visual impairments; students who are deaf or have hearing impairments; and students with intellectual disabilities. (Based on 13 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Write reports to summarize student performance, social growth, or physical development.
Meet with parents or guardians to discuss their children's progress, advise them on using community resources, or teach skills for dealing with students' impairments.
Interpret the results of standardized tests to determine students' strengths and areas of need.
Instruct students in daily living skills required for independent maintenance and self-sufficiency, such as hygiene, safety, and food preparation.
Instruct students with disabilities in academic subjects, using a variety of techniques to reinforce learning and meet students' varying needs.
Guide or counsel students with adjustment problems, academic problems, or special academic interests.
Establish and communicate clear objectives for all lessons, units, and projects to students.
Encourage students to explore learning opportunities or persevere with challenging tasks to prepare them for later grades.
Develop or implement strategies to meet the needs of students with a variety of disabilities.
Coordinate placement of students with special needs into mainstream classes.

Confer with parents, administrators, testing specialists, social workers, or other professionals to develop individual educational plans (IEPs) for students' educational, physical, or social development.
Confer with other staff members to plan or schedule lessons promoting learning, following approved curricula.
Collaborate with other teachers or administrators to develop, evaluate, or revise educational programs for students with special needs.
Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, or teacher training workshops to maintain or improve professional competence.
Administer standardized ability and achievement tests to students with special needs.
Arrange indoor or outdoor space to facilitate creative play, motor-skill activities, or safety.
Attend to children's basic needs by feeding them, dressing them, or changing their diapers.
Communicate nonverbally with children to provide them with comfort, encouragement, or positive reinforcement.
Assess students' physical progress or needs and evaluate the motor needs of individual students to determine their requirement for adapted physical education services.
Modify the general education curriculum for students with disabilities based on a variety of instructional techniques and technology.
Establish and enforce rules for behavior and procedures for maintaining order among students.
Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws, district policies, or administrative regulations.
Teach basic skills, such as color, shape, number and letter recognition, personal hygiene, or social skills, to students with special needs.
Teach socially acceptable behavior, employing techniques such as behavior modification or positive reinforcement.
Provide students with adapted physical education instruction that meets desired physical needs or goals, and provide assistive devices and supportive technology to enhance their school performance.
Prepare lesson plans in accordance with individualized education plans (IEPs) and the functional abilities or needs of students.
Organize and supervise games or other recreational activities to promote physical, mental, or social development.
Observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health.
Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities, and organize and label materials and display students' work.
Plan and supervise experiential learning activities, such as class projects, field trips, or demonstrations, and guide students in learning from those activities.
Instruct and monitor students in the use and care of equipment and materials to prevent injuries and damage.
Develop individualized education plans (IEPs) designed to promote students' educational, physical, or social development.
Provide assistive devices, supportive technology, or assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
Monitor teachers or teacher assistants to ensure adherence to special education program requirements.
Prepare, administer, or grade tests or assignments to evaluate students' progress.
Present information in audio-visual or interactive formats to support classroom learning.
Coordinate the placement of students with special needs into mainstream classes and monitor adherence to inclusive special education program requirements.

Administer standardized ability and achievement tests, interpret results to determine students' strengths and needs, and provide guidance for students and their families regarding educational resources.
Meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress, determine priorities, and provide guidance in using community resources.
Prepare, administer, and grade tests and assignments to evaluate students' progress and prepare lesson plans or learning modules tailored to students' needs and goals.

Guide and counsel students regarding adjustments, academic problems, or special academic interests and provide advice on personal development skills such as goal setting, independence, and self-advocacy.
Observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health, and collaborate with other staff to create individualized education plans (IEPs).
Establish clear objectives for lessons and communicate them effectively to students while employing special educational strategies to support diverse learning needs.
Plan and conduct a balanced program of instruction and activities that provide opportunities for students to observe, question, and investigate within their learning environment.
Prepare for assigned classes, maintain accurate and complete student records, and ensure compliance with educational regulations and curriculum guidelines.
Provide assistive devices and supportive technology to facilitate access for students with disabilities and ensure their participation in all classroom activities.
Instruct students in daily living skills and personal care to enhance their independence and self-sufficiency.
Participate in staff meetings, professional development, and training workshops to improve educational practices and maintain professional competence.
Teach a variety of subjects and social skills, emphasizing communication, conflict resolution, and other interpersonal skills beneficial for students' educational and vocational futures.
Counsel students on educational issues such as course selection, class scheduling, and career planning, and conduct follow-up interviews to assess their progress.
Develop and implement strategies to address the needs of students with a variety of special educational needs and encourage them to explore learning opportunities.
Collaborate with teachers and administrative staff in curriculum development, execution, and evaluation of educational programs, while also advising on materials and methods.
Provide tutoring and individualized support to assist students in improving academic performance and developing effective study skills and strategies.
Organize activities such as field trips, guest speaker visits, and extracurricular programs to enrich students' educational experiences and social development.
Conduct evaluations and ensure compliance with educational standards, and update course content to reflect current educational practices and technologies.
Advise on community resources for students and families, referring them to appropriate support services as needed.
